title: 2025年度 第3回検定一次試験 英検2級 リスニング

parts.name: 第1部
parts.description: 対話を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Bill! I heard you got into Browning College. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Thanks. I'm not sure if I'm going there, though.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Really? Browning College is an excellent school.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, I really want to go to Western College, so I also applied there. I hope to hear back from them sometime this month.

questions.question: What is one thing the boy says?
choices: He graduated from Browning College.
He has never heard of Western College.
He applied to more than one college.
He started college last month.
questions.explanation: 男子はBrowning Collegeに合格したが、Western Collegeにも出願したと言っています。
highlights: I also applied there
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「卒業した」→合格しただけ。選択肢2「聞いたことがない」→出願している。選択肢3「複数の大学に出願した」→正解。選択肢4「先月入学」→まだ返事待ち。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Hi, Jill. It's Eddie—your next-door neighbor.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hi. What's up?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'm on my way to work, and I realized I forgot to close the front gate when I left my house. I'm worried my dog may get out. Could you see if he's still there and close the gate?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: OK. I can see from my window that your dog's still there. But sure, I'll close it for you right now.

questions.question: What will the woman do next?
choices: Feed the man's dog.
Shut the man's gate.
Call the man back.
Visit the man's office.
questions.explanation: 女性は今すぐ門を閉めてあげると言っています。
highlights: I'll close it for you right now
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「犬に餌をやる」→門を閉めるだけ。選択肢2「門を閉める」→正解。選択肢3「電話をかけ直す」→言及なし。選択肢4「オフィスを訪問」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: There are so many people in the park today. I don't remember ever seeing it so busy.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Lots of people started coming here after the new jungle gym was built.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Oh, the one over there? That looks like so much fun. Can adults climb on it, too?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yes. The sign says it's for everyone. Let's try it out. Come on.

questions.question: Why is the park busy today?
choices: Adults can use the park for free.
An outdoor concert is taking place.
The weather is particularly good.
The park has a new attraction.
questions.explanation: 新しいジャングルジムができてから多くの人が来るようになったと言っています。
highlights: after the new jungle gym was built
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「無料で利用できる」→言及なし。選択肢2「コンサート」→言及なし。選択肢3「天気が良い」→言及なし。選択肢4「新しいアトラクション」→正解。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, goodbye, Mr. Evans. Please thank your family again for being so nice to me during my homestay.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I will, Junko. They really liked learning about Japan from you.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I hope you can all come to Japan someday. I'd love to show you around.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: That would be great. I'm sure we'd enjoy it.

questions.question: What does the girl hope that the man and his family will do?
choices: Let her stay with them again.
Visit her in Japan.
Learn Japanese from her.
Write a letter to her family.
questions.explanation: 女の子は日本に来てほしい、案内したいと言っています。
highlights: I hope you can all come to Japan someday
I'd love to show you around
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「また泊めてもらう」→言及なし。選択肢2「日本を訪れる」→正解。選択肢3「日本語を学ぶ」→言及なし。選択肢4「手紙を書く」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: This soup is delicious, Don. It's so good you could probably sell it.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Do you really think so, Anne? I've always dreamed of opening my own restaurant.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: If the rest of the dishes you cook are this good, you might have a successful business on your hands.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, that's the problem. I only know how to make soup. I'd need to learn how to make a variety of dishes.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about the man?
choices: He can only cook one type of dish.
He does not have much money.
He does not enjoy cooking.
He is busy running a business.
questions.explanation: 男性はスープしか作れないと言っています。
highlights: I only know how to make soup
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「一種類の料理しか作れない」→正解。選択肢2「お金がない」→言及なし。選択肢3「料理が好きではない」→レストラン開業が夢。選択肢4「事業で忙しい」→まだ開業していない。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Kevin, could you help me out with something this weekend?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Well, I'll be busy on Sunday. I'm going home to see my mom in Riverton.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Do you think you could come over and help me rearrange some furniture on Saturday afternoon? I just bought a new sofa, and I want to move the table and chairs in my living room.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Sure, I'm free then. See you on Saturday.

questions.question: How will the man help the woman?
choices: By driving her to Riverton.
By moving some furniture.
By going shopping for her.
By selling her his old sofa.
questions.explanation: 女性は家具の配置換えを手伝ってほしいと頼んでいます。
highlights: help me rearrange some furniture
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「Rivertonまで運転」→日曜の予定。選択肢2「家具を動かす」→正解。選択肢3「買い物」→言及なし。選択肢4「ソファを売る」→女性が購入。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Excuse me. You look lost. Do you need help?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yes, thank you. I need to go to the Hobson Towers apartment complex, but the map on my phone is a bit hard to read.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Oh, that's easy. Keep walking straight down this road for about five minutes, and you'll see the apartment building on your right.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Oh, wonderful. I was worried that I was in the wrong place.

questions.question: Why does the man need help?
choices: He is having trouble reading a map.
He is at the wrong apartment building.
He lost the key to his apartment.
He cannot find his phone.
questions.explanation: 男性はスマホの地図が見づらいと言っています。
highlights: the map on my phone is a bit hard to read
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「地図が読みづらい」→正解。選択肢2「間違った建物」→まだ着いていない。選択肢3「鍵をなくした」→言及なし。選択肢4「スマホが見つからない」→持っている。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Dad, you'll be sixty-seven years old next month. Do you ever think about retiring?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: No, not really. I love my job. And if I stop working, I'll need to find a hobby to keep me active, like gardening. I hate having nothing to do.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Well, it's good that you like your work. I hope I feel that way someday, too.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Don't worry, Linda. I'm sure you'll find a job you enjoy.

questions.question: What is one thing we learn about the man?
choices: He is having trouble at work.
He likes being busy.
He stopped working last year.
He works in the garden every day.
questions.explanation: 男性は何もしないのが嫌で、仕事が好きだと言っています。
highlights: I love my job
I hate having nothing to do
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「仕事で困っている」→仕事が好き。選択肢2「忙しいのが好き」→正解。選択肢3「昨年退職」→まだ働いている。選択肢4「毎日庭仕事」→趣味の例として挙げただけ。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: What are you so excited about, Amanda?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I'm going to have a great weekend. My dad is taking me to the Cartoon World theme park on Saturday.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Cartoon World? Wow! I've always wanted to go there. I hear the rides are really fun.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I know. I've been looking forward to it for a long time. I can't wait to meet all my favorite cartoon characters.

questions.question: What is Amanda looking forward to doing this weekend?
choices: Going to a theme park.
Watching cartoons at home.
Taking a bike ride.
Meeting her friends.
questions.explanation: Amandaは土曜日にCartoon Worldテーマパークに行くのを楽しみにしています。
highlights: My dad is taking me to the Cartoon World theme park
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「テーマパークに行く」→正解。選択肢2「家でアニメを見る」→テーマパーク。選択肢3「自転車」→言及なし。選択肢4「友達に会う」→キャラクターに会いたい。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Hello. ABC Travel Agency.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hi. I need to get to Stanton tomorrow morning. Should I buy a train ticket or an express bus ticket?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: The train is much faster. It takes about two and a half hours to get there by train, but it takes five hours by bus.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: I see. Then I'll buy a train ticket.

questions.question: What does the man suggest the woman do?
choices: Buy her tickets online.
Take the express bus.
Leave for Kyoto tonight.
Take the train to Stanton.
questions.explanation: 男性は電車の方がずっと速いと勧めています。
highlights: The train is much faster
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「オンラインで購入」→言及なし。選択肢2「バスに乗る」→電車を勧めている。選択肢3「今夜出発」→明朝の話。選択肢4「電車に乗る」→正解。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Excuse me, ma'am. You look like you're waiting for the bus. Do you need help?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Yes. I was waiting for Express Bus 12, but it doesn't seem to be coming.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: It came five minutes ago. I'm sorry, but it won't come again for another thirty minutes.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Oh, no! I told my boss I would be at the office by 9 a.m. I guess I'm going to be late again.

questions.question: What does the man tell the woman?
choices: The bus will be late.
The bus stop is around the corner.
The bus will be here in five minutes.
The bus has already left.
questions.explanation: 男性はバスが5分前に行ったと伝えています。
highlights: It came five minutes ago
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「バスが遅れる」→既に行った。選択肢2「バス停は角の先」→言及なし。選択肢3「5分で来る」→5分前に行った。選択肢4「バスは既に出発した」→正解。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Jason, have you been to the new Thai restaurant on Fifth Street? I've been there for lunch twice this week.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: No, I haven't. Is the food good?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Yes. It's the best Thai food I've had since I went to Thailand last year.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: That's great. I love Thai food. I'll try the restaurant for lunch today.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the Thai restaurant?
choices: The food is excellent.
The prices are too high.
It is not easy to find.
It has a nice atmosphere.
questions.explanation: 女性は去年タイに行って以来最高のタイ料理だと言っています。
highlights: It's the best Thai food I've had since I went to Thailand last year
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「料理が素晴らしい」→正解。選択肢2「値段が高い」→言及なし。選択肢3「見つけにくい」→言及なし。選択肢4「雰囲気が良い」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I'd like a plate of fried fish and one chef's salad, please.

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Certainly. The fried fish will take about ten minutes to prepare. Shall I bring the salad along with it?

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Yes, I can wait. Please bring them together. I also want to order dessert later, so can you leave the menu with me?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Of course. I'll put your order in right away, then.

questions.question: What does the man want the woman to do?
choices: Bring two items at the same time.
Give him the special dessert menu.
Tell him her recommendation.
Clear the plates from the table.
questions.explanation: 男性は2品を一緒に持ってきてほしいと言っています。
highlights: Please bring them together
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「2品を同時に持ってくる」→正解。選択肢2「デザートメニュー」→通常のメニューを残してほしい。選択肢3「おすすめを教えて」→言及なし。選択肢4「皿を片付ける」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hello. Edwards Bakery.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Hello. I stopped by your bakery this morning to buy some bread, but it was all sold out. What time does the bakery open?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: At 6 a.m. We usually sell out by 8 a.m.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: I see. I'll be sure to come earlier next time.

questions.question: What does the man want to know?
choices: When the bakery has a sale.
When the bakery opens.
When the bakery is hiring.
When the bakery closes.
questions.explanation: 男性はパン屋が何時に開くか聞いています。
highlights: What time does the bakery open?
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「セールの時期」→開店時間。選択肢2「開店時間」→正解。選択肢3「求人時期」→言及なし。選択肢4「閉店時間」→開店時間。

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for a dress for my daughter.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: What kind of dress? A formal one, or something for everyday wear?

lines.speaker: female
lines.text: She needs one for the school play. She's acting in it, so she needs a dress that looks like it's from a hundred years ago.

lines.speaker: male
lines.text: Oh, I see. Well, we have some inexpensive used dresses over there.

questions.question: What does the woman need for her daughter?
choices: A dress that is comfortable.
A dress that looks like it is new.
A dress for the school play.
A dress for everyday wear.
questions.explanation: 女性は学校の劇に使うドレスを探しています。
highlights: She needs one for the school play
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「快適なドレス」→言及なし。選択肢2「新品に見えるドレス」→100年前風。選択肢3「学校の劇用のドレス」→正解。選択肢4「普段着」→劇用。

parts.name: 第2部
parts.description: 英文を聞き、その質問に対して最も適切な答えを1, 2, 3, 4の中から一つ選ぶ形式です。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: In the Netherlands, there is something called the floating solar farm. Solar panels are floating on the water, making it easier to change directions and move. Because of these features, they can follow the sun throughout the day. Another benefit of floating on the water is that it can prevent the rise in temperature, which is a disadvantage of solar panels.

questions.question: How can the floating solar panels get sunlight during the day?
choices: By using a great number of panels.
By catching the light reflected on the water.
By following where the sun goes.
By removing any obstacles in the way.
questions.explanation: 水上に浮かぶことで方向を変えやすく、一日中太陽を追いかけることができます。
highlights: they can follow the sun throughout the day
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「大量のパネル」→言及なし。選択肢2「水面の反射をキャッチ」→言及なし。選択肢3「太陽の動きを追う」→正解。選択肢4「障害物を除去」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Emma wanted to plant sunflower seeds in her garden. She read a book that says that sunflowers need a lot of sunlight, so she chose a spot that gets a lot of sun during the day. She also made sure that the soil in that spot was not too wet or too dry. She planted her seeds and watered them every day. Soon, she had beautiful sunflowers in her garden.

questions.question: How did Emma make sure her sunflowers would grow well?
choices: She kept them indoors.
She watered them every other day.
She used old soil from her garden.
She planted them in a sunny spot.
questions.explanation: Emmaは日当たりの良い場所を選びました。
highlights: she chose a spot that gets a lot of sun during the day
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「室内で育てた」→庭。選択肢2「隔日で水やり」→毎日。選択肢3「古い土」→言及なし。選択肢4「日当たりの良い場所」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Welcome to Brightway Department Store. We have special discounts on many items, including TVs and kitchen appliances. There are also some great deals on men's and women's clothing. If you need help finding a specific section of the store, please visit the information desk on the first floor. Our staff there will be happy to assist you. Thank you for shopping at Brightway.

questions.question: What is one thing the announcement says?
choices: Staff members are needed on the first floor.
The TVs on sale are all sold out.
There are sales on many kinds of products.
The first floor is closed for renovations.
questions.explanation: テレビ、キッチン家電、衣類など多くの商品が割引中です。
highlights: special discounts on many items
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「スタッフ募集」→案内係がいる。選択肢2「テレビ完売」→言及なし。選択肢3「多くの商品がセール中」→正解。選択肢4「1階改装中」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Good afternoon, everyone. Thank you for traveling with us today. We would like to apologize for the delay of the 3:30 train from Ashford to Harrow. It is thirty minutes late because it needs to be cleaned after the long trip from Harrow to Ashford. The train will arrive soon, so please wait on the platform. We are sorry for the inconvenience.

questions.question: Why is the train delayed?
choices: It is being repaired.
The weather was bad in Ashford.
The platform was blocked.
It needs to be cleaned.
questions.explanation: 列車はHarrowからAshfordへの長い旅の後、清掃が必要なため遅延しています。
highlights: it needs to be cleaned after the long trip
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「修理中」→清掃。選択肢2「Ashfordの天気」→言及なし。選択肢3「プラットフォームが塞がっている」→言及なし。選択肢4「清掃が必要」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Welcome to today's Book Talk event. Our guest speaker today is the famous author, Mary Jones. She will read from her new children's book, The Secret of the Missing Cat. Before we start, we would like to remind you that there is a new book section next to the window on the first floor. It has just been opened, so please come and check it out.

questions.question: What is one thing the speaker says?
choices: There is a new book area.
The new book has sold out.
Books about cats have sold well.
The event will be held at Mary's house.
questions.explanation: 1階の窓際に新しい書籍コーナーがオープンしたと案内しています。
highlights: there is a new book section
It has just been opened
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「新しい書籍エリアがある」→正解。選択肢2「新刊が完売」→言及なし。選択肢3「猫の本が売れている」→言及なし。選択肢4「Maryの家で開催」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Emily and Ryan went on a trip to a tropical island. They stayed at a hotel with a large swimming pool and a very good restaurant. However, their favorite part of the trip was exploring the beautiful island and watching the sun set over the ocean. They took many photos and made plans to go back again next year.

questions.question: What did Emily and Ryan enjoy the most about their trip?
choices: Swimming in the hotel's pool.
Taking photos of the hotel's garden.
Looking around the island.
Eating at the hotel's restaurant.
questions.explanation: 二人のお気に入りは美しい島を探索することでした。
highlights: their favorite part of the trip was exploring the beautiful island
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「プールで泳ぐ」→お気に入りではない。選択肢2「庭の写真」→島を探索。選択肢3「島を見て回る」→正解。選択肢4「レストランで食事」→お気に入りではない。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: When it is cold, we do not feel like going outside to do some exercise. However, exercising in cold weather has benefits for us. When we move our bodies in cold weather, our hearts work harder than usual to send blood throughout our bodies. As a result, we can burn more calories and exercise longer. By training in cold conditions, we can also improve our performance in hot weather.

questions.question: What is one benefit of exercising in cold weather?
choices: It helps us make more blood in our bodies.
It helps us perform better in hot weather.
It helps us improve our ability to see at night.
It helps us control our emotions during winter.
questions.explanation: 寒い中でトレーニングすると暑い天候でのパフォーマンスも向上します。
highlights: improve our performance in hot weather
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「血液を増やす」→血液を全身に送る。選択肢2「暑い天候でのパフォーマンス向上」→正解。選択肢3「夜間視力」→言及なし。選択肢4「感情コントロール」→言及なし。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Attention, passengers. The 2:30 p.m. bus to Central City will arrive in five minutes. Please have your tickets ready to show to the driver. Remember that the bus will stop at the Central City Shopping Mall, the Central City Train Station, and the Central City Airport. If you are going to the airport, be sure to get off at the last stop. Thank you.

questions.question: What is one thing the speaker says?
choices: Tickets for the bus cost five dollars.
Tickets should be purchased from the driver.
The bus will arrive in five minutes.
The bus will stop at the airport first.
questions.explanation: バスは5分で到着すると案内しています。
highlights: will arrive in five minutes
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「チケットは5ドル」→5分で到着。選択肢2「運転手からチケット購入」→見せるだけ。選択肢3「5分で到着」→正解。選択肢4「空港に最初に停車」→最後の停留所。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Attention, passengers. The 10:30 flight to London will be leaving from Gate 22. Please make your way to the gate now. Passengers with small children and those needing assistance will be allowed to board the plane first, followed by business class passengers. Economy class passengers will board the plane after that. Thank you for choosing Bluebird Airlines. We hope you enjoy your flight.

questions.question: What is being announced?
choices: The passengers who must check in.
The reason for a delay.
The length of the next flight.
The departure gate of a flight.
questions.explanation: ロンドン行きのフライトはゲート22から出発すると案内しています。
highlights: will be leaving from Gate 22
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「チェックインが必要な乗客」→搭乗案内。選択肢2「遅延の理由」→言及なし。選択肢3「フライト時間」→言及なし。選択肢4「出発ゲート」→正解。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Wendy takes an English vocabulary test at school every week. Her teacher posts a test online, and students access it and take the test. However, her phone broke down this week, and she had to go for one week without a phone. She asked her teacher what she could do and was allowed to take the test on paper this week.

questions.question: What did Wendy's teacher do for Wendy?
choices: He let her take a printed exam.
He gave her a week for an online exam.
He told her to use a school computer.
He allowed her to skip the test this week.
questions.explanation: 先生はWendyに今週は紙でテストを受けることを許可しました。
highlights: was allowed to take the test on paper this week
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「紙のテストを受けさせた」→正解。選択肢2「1週間猶予を与えた」→紙で受けた。選択肢3「学校のPCを使うよう言った」→紙。選択肢4「テストを免除した」→紙で受けた。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Tom has been working as a nurse for five years. He enjoys helping children become healthy again, but he is thinking about changing his job. Tom loves his work, but he feels that he has not been learning new things recently. He is looking for a new job at a hospital where he can get additional training.

questions.question: What is Tom looking for in his next job?
choices: A chance to travel.
An opportunity to learn new things.
An easy way to become healthy.
A job at a school.
questions.explanation: Tomは追加のトレーニングが受けられる病院の仕事を探しています。
highlights: he has not been learning new things recently
where he can get additional training
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「旅行のチャンス」→言及なし。選択肢2「新しいことを学ぶ機会」→正解。選択肢3「健康になる方法」→言及なし。選択肢4「学校の仕事」→病院。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Alex had a school project to do about a famous scientist. He started the project very late, and he was not able to finish it before he went to bed. In the morning, he woke up early and worked on it for a few hours. He was very tired when he got to school, but he was happy that he could give his project to his teacher in class.

questions.question: What did Alex do before he went to bed?
choices: He worked on his school project.
He contacted his teacher.
He turned in his project.
He studied for a science test.
questions.explanation: Alexは寝る前にプロジェクトに取り組みましたが、終わらせることができませんでした。
highlights: He started the project very late
he was not able to finish it before he went to bed
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「プロジェクトに取り組んだ」→正解。選択肢2「先生に連絡」→言及なし。選択肢3「プロジェクトを提出」→朝に完成。選択肢4「理科のテスト勉強」→プロジェクト。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Tom grew up in a small village, but when he finished university, he moved to the city to work. A few years later, his parents called him and said they were having trouble with their farm. Tom went back to the village to help them. He decided to stay and help his parents. Now, he enjoys teaching people from the city about farming.

questions.question: What does Tom do now?
choices: He works in a big city.
He teaches city people about farming.
He runs his own farm.
He goes to university classes.
questions.explanation: Tomは今、都会の人に農業を教えることを楽しんでいます。
highlights: he enjoys teaching people from the city about farming
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「大都市で働く」→村に戻った。選択肢2「都会の人に農業を教えている」→正解。選択肢3「自分の農場を経営」→親の農場。選択肢4「大学に通っている」→卒業済み。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: Thank you for shopping at the Westwood Mall. This weekend, many of our stores are having special sales. You can get discounts of up to 50 percent on selected items. In addition, if you spend more than $100 at any of our stores, you can get a coupon for $10 off your next purchase. Don't miss out on these great offers!

questions.question: What can customers get if they spend over $100?
choices: Discounts on all items.
A coupon worth $10.
A special discount of 50 percent.
A gift worth $10.
questions.explanation: 100ドル以上購入すると、次回の買い物で10ドル割引のクーポンがもらえます。
highlights: you can get a coupon for $10 off your next purchase
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「全商品の割引」→一部商品。選択肢2「10ドルクーポン」→正解。選択肢3「50%割引」→100ドル以上の特典ではない。選択肢4「10ドルの贈り物」→クーポン。

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: John bought his house ten years ago. At that time, he was single. Then, he got married and had a son. Last year, his wife had twins, and they needed more space. Last week, John sold his house and bought a bigger one. It is in the same neighborhood, so his son can still go to the same school.

questions.question: What did John do last week?
choices: He bought a bigger house.
He moved back to his old house.
He went traveling with his wife.
He sold his son's house.
questions.explanation: Johnは先週、家を売ってより大きな家を購入しました。
highlights: John sold his house and bought a bigger one
questions.choiceAnalysis: 選択肢1「大きな家を購入した」→正解。選択肢2「古い家に戻った」→新しい家を購入。選択肢3「旅行に行った」→言及なし。選択肢4「息子の家を売った」→自分の家。
